content.problemStatement:
Chef Go has planted a flower field — a grid with nnn rows and mmm columns. In each cell of this grid, a single flower is planted; let's denote the type of the flower planted in cell (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) by ai,ja_{i,j}ai,j​.

Today, Chef Go is going to make a new menu with two dishes: Tom Yum Goong and Tom Kha Kai. He needs to pick as many flowers as possible to decorate the dishes and make them look attractive. Each dish can only be decorated with flowers of a single type, so he can only pick flowers of one type or two different types. Also, Chef Go wants the flowers he picks to form one connected area, so that he can finish his job quickly. A connected area is a set of cells such that we can move from any cell to any other cell in this set by only moving between side-adjacent cells from the set.

Help Chef Go find the size of the largest connected area in his flower field which contains at most two types of flowers.
constraints: 1 <= ai,j <= 4*10^6 for each valid i and j
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two space-separated integers n and m denoting the size of the field. The next n lines contain m space-separated integers each, representing the type of flower in each cell.
content.outputFormat: The maximum number of flowers that can be picked to form a connected area with at most two types of flowers.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
One connected area with maximum size consists of the following flowers (flowers marked by dots are not picked):
1 1 2 . .
. 1 2 . .
. 2 1 . .
. . 1 2 1
The entire grid is a connected area with the same type of flower.
The entire grid is a connected area with two types of flowers.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a breadth-first search (BFS) algorithm to traverse the graph and find the largest connected area with at most two types of flowers. We will maintain a set of visited cells and a queue of cells to be visited. For each cell, we will check if it is connected to the current cell and if it has the same or a different type of flower. If it is connected and has the same type of flower, we will add it to the queue and mark it as visited. If it is connected and has a different type of flower, we will check if we have already found two types of flowers. If we have, we will skip this cell. If we haven't, we will add it to the queue and mark it as visited. We will keep track of the size of the largest connected area found so far.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n*m) — because in the worst case, we need to visit each cell once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n*m) — because in the worst case, we need to store each cell in the queue and the set of visited cells.
